chinese_text: 用兵有言﹕「吾不敢為主而為客，
translation_text: A master of the art of war has said, 'I do not dare to be the host (to commence the war); I prefer to be the guest (to act on the defensive).
translation_text_raw: A master of the art of war has said, 'I do not dare to be the host (to commence the war); I prefer to be the guest (to act on the defensive).

chinese_text: 不敢進寸而退尺。」是謂行無行，
translation_text: I do not dare to advance an inch; I prefer to retire a foot.' This is called marshalling the ranks where there are no ranks;
translation_text_raw: I do not dare to advance an inch; I prefer to retire a foot.' This is called marshalling the ranks where there are no ranks;

translation_text: baring the arms (to fight) where there are no arms to bare;
translation_text_raw: baring the arms (to fight) where there are no arms to bare;

translation_text: grasping the weapon where there is no weapon to grasp;
translation_text_raw: grasping the weapon where there is no weapon to grasp;

translation_text: advancing against the enemy where there is no enemy. There is no calamity greater than lightly engaging in war.
translation_text_raw: advancing against the enemy where there is no enemy. There is no calamity greater than lightly engaging in war.

translation_text: To do that is near losing (the gentleness) which is so precious.
translation_text_raw: To do that is near losing (the gentleness) which is so precious.

translation_text: Thus it is that when opposing weapons are (actually) crossed, he who deplores (the situation) conquers.
translation_text_raw: Thus it is that when opposing weapons are (actually) crossed, he who deplores (the situation) conquers.
